title: Unique Paths in a Grid
hints: Use dynamic programming with a 2D dp array where dp[i][j] represents the number of unique paths to reach (i, j).
If the starting cell (0,0) or ending cell (n-1, m-1) has an obstacle, return 0 immediately.
Initialize dp[0][0] = 1 if there's no obstacle at the start.
For the first row, you can only come from the left, so dp[0][j] = dp[0][j-1] if A[0][j] == 0, else 0.
For the first column, you can only come from the top, so dp[i][0] = dp[i-1][0] if A[i][0] == 0, else 0.
For other cells, if there's no obstacle, dp[i][j] = dp[i-1][j] + dp[i][j-1] (can come from top or left).
If a cell has an obstacle, set dp[i][j] = 0.
